title: The breath as a wave settling sand
subtitle: A breath-anchored visualisation for slowing the body into sleep using the image of slow, rhythmic water.
excerpt: A short visualisation for Day 9 of the SLEEP program. Each breath becomes a slow wave moving through the body, settling what it touches. The image gives the breath a rhythm to follow.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. Developed as a companion practice to Day 9 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

content.text: A short visualisation for Day 9 of the SLEEP program. The practice uses one image (breath as a wave) to give the mind a point of focus and allow the body to slow into rest.
Lie down. Close your eyes. Take one breath without trying to change it, just notice it.

Now imagine each breath as a slow wave moving across a shallow beach. On the inhale, the wave comes in, long and unhurried, from your feet up through your belly and chest. On the exhale, it draws back out again, leaving the sand a little smoother each time.
Follow three waves in, three waves out. Let the image slow the breath rather than forcing the breath to match an image.
Notice where in the body the wave settles on the exhale. Where the tension goes soft. Shoulders. Jaw. Belly.
Continue for as many waves as it takes. There is no destination other than the next breath.
Original to homemade.education. Developed as a companion practice to Day 9 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
